location.office_time_required: flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office with the ability to travel as needed

level_signals: 2+ years in a sales role focused on building relationships and closing deals
1+ years of experience in Outbound Sales
Reporting to an Inside Sales Manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years in a sales role focused on building relationships and closing deals
You have 1+ years of experience in Outbound Sales being held to DTT minimums
You are tech-savvy and navigate sales and internal tools quickly (Salesforce, Outreach, Sales Navigator, Google Apps)
Make a high volume of calls and emails to existing partners daily
You are comfortable working in a scaling startup environment and adapting quickly
You are creative, motivated, and passionate about sales and have a dynamic personality

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience working with Franchise accounts (nice to have)

scope.surface: Inside Account Development — upselling Ads, Loyalty & Promotions to existing SMB merchant partners
scope.business_model: B2B2C — SaaS-like upsell of DoorDash platform products (Ads, Loyalty, Promotions) to SMB merchant partners
core_responsibilities: Upsell incremental products and services (Ads, Loyalty & Promotions) to existing merchants
Make high volume of calls and emails to existing partners daily
Collaborate with Inside Account Management Team to achieve goals of Managed Partners
Activate additional products on the DoorDash platform to empower merchant partner growth
Cultivate unique solutions through data and analytical tools
Use email marketing automation tools to creatively engage partners
Connect and collaborate with existing partners to understand their unique goals, priorities, and challenges

green_flags: Specific product focus (Ads, Loyalty & Promotions) gives clear scope to the role
Hybrid flexibility explicitly stated — not strictly on-site
Inclusive hiring language and strong DEI commitments stated
Well-known brand with clear growth trajectory
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed anywhere in the JD
Bilingual Spanish is in the title as 'Required' but not explicitly called out as a hard requirement in the body — could cause confusion
DTT minimums referenced without defining what they are — ambiguous performance bar

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and reporting structure)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and merchant partner context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and DTT reference; not explicitly stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office' language)
ambiguities: Bilingual Spanish appears in the role title as 'Required' but is not listed explicitly as a bullet under 'We're excited about you because…' — treated as hard requirement based on title

Location type: JD says 'flexible in terms of time spent remote and in-office' which implies hybrid, but the team description emphasizes Phoenix, AZ. Treated as hybrid with Phoenix as primary location.
DTT minimums mentioned as a performance metric but never defined in the JD
